pi_items.source: GeoServer official documentation - WFS (Web Feature Service) is a standard OWS service for web-based access to geographic features
GeoServer WFS reference documentation confirms Transaction is a valid WFS operation for creating, modifying, and deleting geographic features
GeoServer WFS reference documentation confirms GetFeature is a valid WFS operation for reading/querying geographic features

pi_items.verified_value: ROLE_ADMINISTRATOR (standard GeoServer) or custom role ADMIN (task-specific)
pi_items.source: GeoServer documentation shows standard role names use ROLE_ prefix (e.g., ROLE_ADMINISTRATOR, ROLE_WFS_WRITE). However, export_result.sh checks for 'ADMIN' role in services.properties, indicating this task expects a custom role name without the prefix.
setup_geoserver.sh script (line 114) imports Natural Earth countries data as table 'ne_countries', published in workspace 'ne' (line 185), resulting in layer 'ne:ne_countries'

pi_items.verified_value: Natural Earth public domain dataset from naciscdn.org/naturalearth and naturalearth.s3.amazonaws.com
pi_items.source: install_geoserver.sh (lines 62-64) downloads from https://naciscdn.org/naturalearth/110m/cultural/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.zip - Natural Earth is public domain free data

privileged_info_summary: The task configures GeoServer 2.25.2 to restrict WFS Transaction operations while allowing public GetFeature access. The target layer is ne:ne_countries from the Natural Earth public domain dataset (not synthetic). WFS and its Transaction/GetFeature methods are verified GeoServer operations. The target role 'ADMIN' is unverified as standard GeoServer uses ROLE_ prefixed role names, though the task may define a custom role. The Natural Earth data is downloaded from public sources (naciscdn.org/naturalearth) and is free to use without attribution.
